claim: Opus 4.8 achieves 69.2% on SweBench Pro, a 5.1-point improvement over Opus 4.7 released 6 weeks prior
actionable: Use Opus 4.8 as preferred coding model for agentic workflows

claim: Dynamic workflows in Claude Code enables tens to hundreds of parallel sub-agents in single session for complex tasks
actionable: Enable dynamic workflows for tasks like codebase-wide migrations, bug hunts, or stress-testing plans

claim: Anthropic released Mythos class model (higher intelligence than Opus) as preview, general availability expected within weeks
actionable: Prepare integrations for Mythos model which will have stronger reasoning and coding capabilities

claim: Claude fast mode is now 3x cheaper than before, trading 2.5x speed increase for 2x price premium
actionable: Use fast mode for latency-sensitive agent tasks, accepting 2x cost for 2.5x speedup

claim: Deep SWE benchmark (70% for GPT-5.5, 54% for Opus 4.7) more accurately reflects real-world coding performance than SweBench
actionable: Use Deep SWE or similar real-world coding benchmarks to evaluate models for production agentic coding
